rationale: Both titles cover the same study and are well-matched for JICE. Title X is more informative: it specifies the imaging modality (MRI-based assessment) and the exact sample size (353 patients), which adds concrete methodological grounding and helps readers immediately gauge study scope. Title Y omits both details, reducing informativeness. Title X's only minor weakness is slight wordiness ('ostial cross-sectional area' is precise but adds length). Title Y is slightly cleaner syntactically — replacing 'ostial' with 'pulmonary vein' cross-sectional area and using 'cohort study' as the study design tag — but the gain in brevity comes at the cost of specificity. For a clinical research audience, the richer detail in X provides more value.
loser_weakness: Title Y omits the MRI-based imaging method and the sample size (353 patients), leaving the reader with less concrete information about the study's scope and methodology. 'Cohort study' as a terminal descriptor is generic and adds little beyond what Title X conveys with its explicit numbers and imaging qualifier.
